skill.name: arrange
skill.description: Improve layout, spacing, and visual rhythm. Fixes monotonous grids, inconsistent spacing, and weak visual hierarchy to create intentional compositions.
skill.summary: Improve layout spacing, hierarchy, rhythm, and structure for more intentional interface compositions.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives from Markdown design guidance, not executable code. Inline CSS examples, layout terminology, and words such as weak were misclassified as shell execution, reconnaissance, and cryptographic risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Misread as External Commands
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick locations are inline Markdown examples for CSS tokens, gap, clamp, flex-wrap, repeat, and grid-template-areas. No evidence found of shell execution or command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain Markdown formatting and CSS layout examples only. There is no Ruby file, script block, subprocess call, or user-controlled command path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Wording Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The high-severity cryptography alerts match ordinary design prose, including weak hierarchy and layout guidance. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language UI design instructions. None include cryptographic APIs, algorithms, encoded data, secrets, or security-sensitive processing.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Layout Terms Misread as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts match interface layout terms such as grid, system, and structure. No evidence found of host inspection, file listing, environment probing, or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is UI layout assessment and responsive design guidance. There are no commands or instructions for collecting system, network, account, or environment information.

content.value_statement: Crowded or monotonous interfaces make content harder to scan and understand. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code improve spacing, hierarchy, grids, and visual rhythm.
actual_capabilities: Assesses spacing consistency, density, hierarchy, grid structure, and visual rhythm.
Plans layout improvements using spacing scales, grouping, responsive grids, and flex layouts.
Guides use of gap, clamp, Flexbox, CSS Grid, and named grid areas.
Identifies repetitive card grids, nested cards, arbitrary spacing, and excessive centering.
Defines verification checks for hierarchy, responsiveness, breathing room, and layout consistency.
limitations: It depends on the separate frontend-design skill for full preparation guidance.
It provides design instructions and does not include executable code or scripts.
It cannot judge final visual quality without access to the target interface.
It focuses on spatial composition, not branding, typography, or color systems.

use_cases.title: Refine a Crowded Product Screen
use_cases.description: Review a dense app view and reorganize spacing so related controls are grouped clearly.

use_cases.title: Improve a Frontend Implementation
use_cases.description: Update a page layout with consistent gaps, responsive structure, and clearer hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Audit a Marketing Page
use_cases.description: Find repetitive sections, weak rhythm, and card grid overuse before launch.

prompt_templates.title: Review Basic Spacing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use arrange to review this screen for spacing consistency, grouping, and crowded areas.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Layout Improvements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use arrange to create a step-by-step layout plan for this page, including spacing, hierarchy, and rhythm.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Repetitive Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Use arrange to reduce card grid monotony and create a stronger responsive layout for this interface.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Responsive Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use arrange to evaluate whether Flexbox, Grid, gaps, and breakpoints fit this layout across screen sizes.

output_examples.input: A dashboard uses equal spacing everywhere and every section is a card.
output: Remove unnecessary card wrappers where spacing can create structure.

output_examples.input: A landing page feels repetitive because all sections use identical centered grids.
output: Introduce asymmetric alignment where it supports scanning.
Vary section spacing to create rhythm.
Use cards only for distinct, actionable content.

output_examples.input: A responsive page breaks awkwardly on medium screens.
output: Use Flexbox for simple rows and wrapped groups.
Use Grid where rows and columns need shared structure.
Verify hierarchy and spacing at each viewport size.

best_practices: Start by identifying the primary content, secondary content, and major groups.
Use a defined spacing scale instead of arbitrary margin and padding values.
Verify the final layout with hierarchy, rhythm, density, and responsiveness checks.
anti_patterns: Use the same spacing between every element, group, and section.
Wrap unrelated content in nested cards to force structure.
Default to CSS Grid when a simpler Flexbox layout is enough.

faq.question: What does this skill improve?
faq.answer: It improves layout spacing, visual hierarchy, rhythm, density, and responsive structure.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: It gives design and implementation guidance, but the file does not include scripts or executable code.

faq.question: Can it help with responsive layouts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Flexbox, CSS Grid, responsive grids, and layout verification.

faq.question: Is this mainly for designers or developers?
faq.answer: It is useful for both. Designers can audit composition, and developers can improve frontend layout structure.

faq.question: Does it cover colors and typography?
faq.answer: Only indirectly. The main focus is spatial composition, grouping, hierarchy, and rhythm.

faq.question: Does it require another skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the assistant to use the frontend-design skill for preparation guidance.
